current_entries.en_definition: An ancient maritime town of Bithynia, in Asia Minor in modern Turkey, almost directly opposite Byzantium.
A piece of chalk, or nowadays processed compressed gypsum (calcium sulfate, CaSO4), that is used for drawing and for writing on a blackboard (chalkboard).

A charred substance.
A charlady, a woman employed to do housework; cleaning lady.
A two-wheeled horse-drawn cart, used in Bronze Age and Early Iron Age warfare.
A light (four-wheeled) carriage used for ceremonial or pleasure purposes.
A en given name.
(astronomy) The largest of the five moons of Pluto.
A person employed to drive a private motor car or a hired car of executive or luxury class (like a limousine).
(chess) Word called out by the victor when making a move that wins the game.
(chess) The conclusive victory in a game of chess that occurs when an opponent's king is threatened with unavoidable capture.
(idiomatic) Tightly packed.
An encouragement to somebody who seems unhappy.
(photography) Said while being photographed, to give the impression of smiling.

examples.english: We make milk into cheese and butter.

Any particular variety of cheese.
Of or relating to chemistry.

current_entries.en_definition: (UK, Commonwealth, Ireland) A written order directing a bank to pay money to a person or entity.
The fruit of the coffee plant, containing the seeds or beans.

current_entries.en_definition: Elegant, stylish.
A large city, the county seat of Cook County, in northeastern Illinois, United States, located on Lake Michigan.

current_entries.en_definition: A country in South America. Official name: Republic of Chile. Capital and largest city: Santiago.
The bottom of a face, (specifically) the typically jutting jawline below the mouth.
(colloquial) Be happy; cheer up.
A narrow opening such as a fissure or crack.

A cutting tool used to remove parts of stone, wood or metal by pushing or pounding the back when the sharp edge is against the material. It consists of a slim, oblong block of metal with a sharp wedge or bevel formed on one end and sometimes a handle at the other end; there are hand tool versions (the original type) and versions as bits for power tools.
(organic compound) A halogenated hydrocarbon, trichloromethane, CHCl3; it is a volatile, sweet-smelling liquid, used extensively as a solvent and formerly as an anesthetic.
One selection or preference; that which is chosen or decided; the outcome of a decision.

current_entries.en_definition: A cut of meat, often containing a section of a rib.

current_entries.en_definition: Of, relating to, written for, or performed by a choir or a chorus.
(intransitive) To laugh with a chortle or chortles.
An Abrahamic religion originating from the community of the followers of Jesus Christ.

(originally, Christianity) A festival or holiday commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ and incorporating various Christian, pre-Christian, pagan, and secular customs, which in Western Christianity is celebrated on December 25 (Christmas Day) in most places.
A written account of events and when they happened, ordered by time.
(cooking) Meat from the shoulder of a cow or other animal.
(intransitive, archaic) To make the sound of a chicken; to cluck.

An agglutinative language of the Bolgar branch of the Turkic language family and is spoken west of the Ural mountains in central Russia. Chuvash is the native language of the Chuvash people and an official language of Chuvashia. It is spoken by about two million people.
The Roman statesman and orator Mārcus Tullius Cicerō (106–43 BC).
(informal) Something that is very easy to do.
The circumference of, or distance around, any space; the measure of a line around an area.

In the shape of, or moving in a circle.

current_entries.en_definition: (dated) A cleric or clergyman (the legal title for clergy of the Church of England is "Clerk in Holy Orders", still used in legal documents and cherished by some of their number).
(euphemistic) The culmination of sexual pleasure, an orgasm.
Something which clips or grasps; a device for attaching one object to another.

A place, especially a monastery or convent, devoted to religious seclusion.
Near.
a small room or enclosed space used for storing clothes or other household items

(US, Philippines, dated, informal) A young woman who attends college.
The edge of the land where it meets an ocean, sea, gulf, bay, or large lake. [from 14th c.]

The cry of a rooster.
Of the same age or era; contemporary.

current_entries.en_definition: (grammar) The degree of comparison of an adjective or adverb used when comparing two entities in terms of a certain property or a certain way of doing something. In English, the comparative of superiority is formed by adding the suffix -er or the word more (e.g. bigger, more fully); the comparative of equality, by adding the word as (e.g. as big, as fully); the comparative of inferiority, by adding the word less (e.g. less big, less fully).

examples.chinese: 我的专业是宗教学。

Any non-soap cleaning agent, especially a synthetic surfactant.
An abugida script used to write many languages originating in India and Nepal, including Sanskrit, Hindi, Marathi, Kashmiri, Sindhi, Maithili, Bhili, Konkani, Bhojpuri, and Nepali.
Characterized by deviation from an expectation or a social standard.
(transitive) To use one’s intellect to plan or design (something).
Fanatically opposing progress or reform.
a measurement of length, width, height, or another spatial direction

examples.english: Check the dimensions of the room before buying furniture.
examples.chinese: 購買家具前先量度房間的尺寸。

current_entries.en_definition: a particular aspect or feature of a situation

examples.english: The decision has an important social dimension.
examples.chinese: 這項決定具有重要的社會層面。

current_entries.en_definition: Specifically, a small natural depression on the skin, especially on the face near the corners of the mouth.
A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
(transitive, colloquial) To deduct, as points, from (somebody), in the manner of a penalty; to penalize.

examples.english: Ding and Xueyou are sitting and listening to Mr. Wang.
examples.chinese: 小丁和学友坐着听王老师说话。

current_entries.en_definition: (informal, British) Tiny and cute; small and attractive.

(transitive) To lower (a flag), particularly a national ensign, to a partially hoisted position in order to render or to return a salute. While lowered, the flag is said to be “at the dip.” A flag being carried on a staff may be dipped by leaning it forward at an approximate angle of 45 degrees.
An authoritative decision from an official body, which may or may not have binding force.
Having a disability.

examples.english: A sprained ankle disabled him from walking for a month.
examples.chinese: 他把足踝扭傷了，整整一個月不能走路。

current_entries.en_definition: A setback or handicap.

examples.english: The comparison between the two was to his disadvantage.
examples.chinese: 两相比较，他那边的形势更不利一些。

current_entries.en_definition: (countable, slightly, dated) Clipping of discotheque (“nightclub for dancing”).
A break or interruption in an existing connection, continuum, or process; disconnection.
Lack of concord, agreement, harmony; disaccord.

examples.english: Finally, the discord between them came to an end.
examples.chinese: 他們的衝突終於平息了。

current_entries.en_definition: Tension or strife resulting from a lack of agreement; dissension.
Any harsh noise, or confused mingling of sounds.
(music) An inharmonious combination of simultaneously sounded tones; a dissonance.
A reduction in price.
(intransitive, construed with against) To make decisions harmful to (a person or group) based on prejudice.
(British spelling) To show lack of favour or antipathy towards.
An intense dislike or loathing someone feels for something bad or nasty.
The act or process of disenchanting or freeing from a false belief or illusion.
(transitive) To have a feeling of aversion or antipathy towards; not to like. [from 16th c.]

examples.english: I dislike being alone.
examples.chinese: 我不喜歡獨處。

current_entries.en_definition: A sudden or complete loss of courage and firmness in the face of trouble or danger; overwhelming and disabling terror; a sinking of the spirits
(gymnastics) The part of a routine in which the gymnast detaches from an apparatus.
Absence of order; state of not being arranged in an orderly manner.

examples.english: His room was in disorder.
examples.chinese: 他的房间乱七八糟。

current_entries.en_definition: (medicine, countable) A physical or mental malfunction.
A lack of respect, esteem or courteous behaviour.
(military) The amount of space between soldiers or cavalry riders marching or standing in a rank; also, the amount of space between such ranks.

examples.english: I can't judge distance.
examples.chinese: 我无法判断距离。

current_entries.en_definition: Lack of trust or confidence.
(US, informal) A duplicate or copy of a document, particularly one created by a spirit duplicator.
(US, nautical) A fixed structure attached to shore to which a vessel is secured when in port; usually for loading and unloading.
A burdock plant, or the leaves of that plant.
The title of an academic or medical doctor; used before or instead of the doctor's name.

examples.english: I will find you a good doctor.
examples.chinese: 我會幫你找個好醫生。

current_entries.en_definition: A person who has attained a doctorate, such as a Ph.D. or Th.D. or one of many other terminal degrees conferred by a college or university.
An original or official paper used as the basis, proof, or support of anything else, including any writing, book, or other instrument conveying information pertinent to such proof or support.

examples.english: I hadn't recognized the importance of this document until you told me about it.
examples.chinese: 你告訴了我以後，我才知道這份文件有多重要。

current_entries.en_definition: A film, TV program, publication etc. which presents a social, political, scientific or historical subject in a factual or informative manner.

examples.english: That documentary about the environmental crisis was a real eye-opener.
examples.chinese: 這部有關環境危機的紀錄片真是令人大開眼界。

current_entries.en_definition: Any of about 100-170 species of yellow, orange or red (rarely green) parasitic plants of the genus Cuscuta. Formerly treated as the only genus in the family Cuscutaceae, it is now placed in the morning glory family, Convolvulaceae.
A fight between dogs, especially as part of the blood sport of dogfighting.
Ruling; governing; prevailing
A famous 1605–1615 Spanish novel by Miguel de Cervantes (1547–1616), whose full title is El ingenioso hidalgo don Quijote de la Mancha (The Ingenious Gentleman Don Quixote of La Mancha).
(countable, Ireland) A darling, a cutie.
Disbelief or uncertainty (about something); (countable) a particular instance of such disbelief or uncertainty.

examples.english: People who love doubt nothing, or doubt everything.
examples.chinese: 喜欢的人什么都不怀疑，或什么都疑神疑鬼。

current_entries.en_definition: A jet or current of water or vapour directed upon some part of the body to benefit it medicinally; in particular, such a jet directed at the vagina for irrigation.
(chiefly, woodworking) To unite (components) with a dovetail (noun sense 2.1) or similar joint.
Sad or pessimistic.
Completely, wholly.
(intransitive) To sleep lightly or briefly; to nap, snooze.

(by extension) Originally (historical) , a soldier armed with a dragoon musket (sense 1.1) who fought both on foot and mounted on a horse; now, a cavalier or horse soldier from a regiment formerly armed with such muskets.
A variety of pigeon, originally a cross between a horseman and a tumbler.
(chiefly, US, Canada) A conduit allowing liquid to flow out of an otherwise contained volume; a plughole (UK)
(intransitive) To move slowly, especially pushed by currents of water, air, etc.

examples.english: Many people drift through life without a purpose.
examples.chinese: 许多人毫无目的地随波逐流。
